introSection: The Flash: Vertical Velocity is a steel launched shuttle inverted roller coaster located at Six Flags Discovery Kingdom in Vallejo, California, USA. It was built by Intamin and opened on June 8, 2001 as V²: Vertical Velocity.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Six Flags Marine World announced the addition of V²: Vertical Velocity in early 2001.

The ride was set to open on May 24, 2001, but the opening was delayed to June 8.

When V² first opened, it was tall and both sides of the track were vertical. However, this violated the city of Vallejo's height limit of. Even though Six Flags Marine World advertised the ride's height as, authorities could tell that the ride was in clear violation of the height limit. Six Flags had to either modify the ride's height, or remove V² completely. Eventually, the park chose to modify the ride. During the 2002 season, the ride was temporarily closed while the track was shortened from to. To lower the height, the front tower was tilted to a 45-degree angle, creating the first twisted in-line rollback in the world, and the reverse was tower lowered to while still being vertical. These modifications were carried out by Valley Iron. V² then reopened in the summer of 2002.

For the 2007 season, V² was repainted with red track and maroon supports. Originally, the ride had yellow track and blue supports.

For part of the 2016 season, V² was closed for unknown reasons.

For the 2019 season, the ride became part of a new area called DC Universe and the name was changed to The Flash: Vertical Velocity. The supports were repainted once again, this time to yellow.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Red track and yellow supports. The coaster opened with yellow track and blue supports, later being repainted to featured orange track and purple supports in 2007 before receiving its current scheme in 2019.

fullText:
The Flash: Vertical Velocity is a steel launched shuttle inverted roller coaster located at Six Flags Discovery Kingdom in Vallejo, California, USA. It was built by Intamin and opened on June 8, 2001 as V²: Vertical Velocity.

Six Flags Marine World announced the addition of V²: Vertical Velocity in early 2001.

The ride was set to open on May 24, 2001, but the opening was delayed to June 8.

When V² first opened, it was tall and both sides of the track were vertical. However, this violated the city of Vallejo's height limit of. Even though Six Flags Marine World advertised the ride's height as, authorities could tell that the ride was in clear violation of the height limit. Six Flags had to either modify the ride's height, or remove V² completely. Eventually, the park chose to modify the ride. During the 2002 season, the ride was temporarily closed while the track was shortened from to. To lower the height, the front tower was tilted to a 45-degree angle, creating the first twisted in-line rollback in the world, and the reverse was tower lowered to while still being vertical. These modifications were carried out by Valley Iron. V² then reopened in the summer of 2002.

For the 2007 season, V² was repainted with red track and maroon supports. Originally, the ride had yellow track and blue supports.

For part of the 2016 season, V² was closed for unknown reasons.

For the 2019 season, the ride became part of a new area called DC Universe and the name was changed to The Flash: Vertical Velocity. The supports were repainted once again, this time to yellow.



Red track and yellow supports. The coaster opened with yellow track and blue supports, later being repainted to featured orange track and purple supports in 2007 before receiving its current scheme in 2019.
